hnote_item_addressed: Item 3 — Lightweight 4-row falsification module on Overview
prerequisites_satisfied: EXP-255 (Items 1+2+5) delivered 2026-04-26T02:10:46Z, awaiting curmudgeon review. EXP-256 insertion anchor (the generateVerdictBarChart call line) is unaffected by EXP-255, so EXP-256 can integrate before, after, or alongside EXP-255 without conflict.
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — insert a new <section class="falsifiability-module"> immediately after the generateVerdictBarChart() call in the Overview tab template (current line 1364), and add a corresponding CSS block immediately after the .accuracy-math-section @media rule (current line 533). The module is a 4-row "What would change our verdict" persistent surface drawn from existing eg-falsifiability content in the Evaluation Guide. INDEPENDENT of EXP-190 (parked); does not depend on EXP-190 schema work.

audit_findings_eg_falsifiability.location: docs/index.html lines 532-557 (rendered from the Evaluation Guide tab content). Section id="eg-falsifiability" titled "What Would Change Our Verdict".
audit_findings_eg_falsifiability.four_conditions_verbatim_match: YES — the four conditions in the existing eg-falsifiability section match the operator-specified module rows one-for-one: (1) "Derive a discriminating prediction from dome geometry" — operator: "A discriminating prediction from dome geometry"; (2) "Produce a genuine prospective prediction" — operator: "A genuine prospective prediction"; (3) "Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry" — operator: same; (4) "Produce a coordinate system that achieves sub-5% error without globe inputs" — operator: "Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs". No invented conditions; faithful distillation.

condition_1.source: eg-falsifiability current text states "only 2 of 69 claims produce a quantitative dome-specific derivation (WIN-044, WIN-057), and both fail". Number that meets the full bar (derive + differ + confirm) = 0. Operator spec: "0 / 69". Match.

condition_2.source: eg-falsifiability text: "The dome's August 2026 eclipse predictions approach this standard but have methodological issues". Operator spec: "1 partial — eclipse 2026". Match. Sub-status pinpoints the date for a casual reader.

condition_3.source: eg-falsifiability text: "If someone can derive 7.83 Hz from dome parameters without smuggling in the globe's radius..." — currently no such derivation exists. Operator spec: "0 / 69". Match.

condition_4.source: eg-falsifiability text references V12/V13 taking WGS84 input and curve-fitting. Section 1.3 confirms OPEN-001 ("Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 (IN PROGRESS)"). Operator spec: "pending". OPEN-001 sub-status grounds the pending state in the dome's own open-problems registry.

audit_findings_eg_falsifiability.denominator_choice: counts.total (= baseWins.length) is used for conditions 1 and 3. This matches the existing eg-falsifiability text ("of 69 claims"). counts.total is currently 69 dome-claimed wins; the verdict bar chart above uses wins.length (= 71 catalog entries including 2 numbered-collision sub-claims). Two different denominators because they answer different questions: the bar chart shows "what landed across all catalog entries"; the falsification module shows "how many of the dome's claimed wins meet the discriminating-prediction bar". Cross-page denominator distinction intentional and consistent with §6.5 prose.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique on its own line — grep -c on the literal string returns 1; the line currently sits at ~1364 and is unaffected by EXP-255 hero swap (which modifies the .scorecard sc-hero block immediately below this line)
patch_segments.insert_text:
What would change our verdict

Four concrete conditions — any one is sufficient. Full criteria →

  1. Discriminating prediction from dome geometry
    Derive a quantitative result from dome parameters that differs from standard physics for the same observable, then confirm it with measurement.
    0 / ${counts.total}
  2. Genuine prospective prediction
    Specific, quantitative, station-named, timestamped before the data — with the prediction document anchored independently from any observation file.
    1 partialeclipse Aug 12, 2026
  3. Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry
    Reproduce the Schumann fundamental from dome parameters alone — no globe radius, no spherical-cavity eigenvalue formula.
    0 / ${counts.total}
  4. Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs
    Reproduce city-to-city distances from dome geometry alone — no WGS84 input, no curve-fitting against globe-measured distances.
    pendingOPEN-001
patch_segments.collision_note: EXP-255 (overview-hero-rework) edits the four-up scorecard sc-hero block IMMEDIATELY after this anchor. Both EXP-255 and EXP-256 insert content after the same anchor but in different positions: EXP-256 inserts the falsifiability-module block, EXP-255 inserts a new thesis-hero / removes the scorecard. If EXP-256 is integrated FIRST: the falsifiability-module block sits between the bar chart and the still-present scorecard sc-hero — visually fine, EXP-255 will then replace the scorecard with its thesis-hero leaving the falsifiability-module unchanged. If EXP-255 is integrated FIRST: the thesis-hero replaces the scorecard, and EXP-256 then inserts the falsifiability-module between the bar chart and the new thesis-hero — also visually fine, in fact arguably better composition. Decider choice on order; recommend integrating EXP-255 first (it is the highest-priority item per HNOTE) and EXP-256 second.

patch_segments.anchor_uniqueness: verified unique — grep -c on the literal string returns 1; the string is the closing brace of the .accuracy-math-section @media rule, currently at line 533
patch_segments.insert_text: .falsifiability-module{margin:1.4rem 0 1.6rem;padding:0.95rem 1.05rem 1rem;border:1px solid var(--border);border-left:3px solid var(--accent);border-radius:6px;background:var(--card-bg)}.falsifiability-module .fm-heading{font-family:var(--sans);font-size:0.82rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.06em;color:var(--ink-3);margin:0 0 0.2rem;font-weight:600;border:none;padding:0}.falsifiability-module .fm-caption{font-family:var(--sans);font-size:0.85rem;color:var(--ink-2);margin:0 0 0.7rem}.falsifiability-module .fm-caption .fm-jump{color:var(--accent);text-decoration:none;font-weight:600}.falsifiability-module .fm-caption .fm-jump:hover,.falsifiability-module .fm-caption .fm-jump:focus-visible{text-decoration:underline}.falsifiability-module .fm-rows{list-style:none;margin:0;padding:0}.falsifiability-module .fm-row{display:grid;grid-template-columns:minmax(11rem,1fr) minmax(0,1.6fr) auto;gap:0.55rem 1.1rem;align-items:baseline;padding:0.6rem 0;border-top:1px solid var(--border)}.falsifiability-module .fm-row:first-child{border-top:none;padding-top:0.2rem}.falsifiability-module .fm-condition{font-family:var(--sans);font-weight:600;color:var(--heading);font-size:0.95rem;line-height:1.35}.falsifiability-module .fm-status{display:inline-flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:flex-end;gap:0.05rem;justify-self:end;min-width:6rem;text-align:right}.falsifiability-module .fm-status-sub{font-family:var(--sans);font-size:0.7rem;color:var(--ink-3);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.05em}.falsifiability-module .fm-status-zero .fm-status-num{color:var(--ink-3)}@media(max-width:720px){.falsifiability-module .fm-row{grid-template-columns:1fr auto;grid-template-areas:"cond status" "desc desc";gap:0.3rem 0.6rem}.falsifiability-module .fm-condition{grid-area:cond}.falsifiability-module .fm-status{grid-area:status;align-items:flex-end;min-width:0}.falsifiability-module .fm-desc{grid-area:desc;font-size:0.86rem}}
patch_segments.design_token_audit: All colors routed through existing CSS variables. fm-status-zero uses --refuted-solid (verdict-encoded red, same as Refuted bar in EXP-209 chart). fm-status-partial uses --accent (matches eclipse badge from EXP-255). fm-status-pending uses --ink-3 (neutral, indicating "no verdict yet"). Border-left uses --accent for a quiet but visible left rail. No hardcoded hex values. Dark mode inherits via :root variable swap at line 497.

word_count_summary.affected_lines_estimate: ~25 lines added (HTML) + 1 long CSS line added

cross_references.falls_back_to: eg-falsifiability section in Evaluation Guide tab — fm-jump anchor links readers to the full criteria with all the prose context (existing condition explanations, eclipse pre-commitment paragraph, etc.)
cross_references.parallels: EXP-255 thesis-hero + verdict-bar-chart sequence — the falsification module sits below the bar chart and forms a triptych with thesis (what we found), distribution (how it broke down), and falsification criteria (what would change it). The Overview now reads as a complete claim/result/standard tile.
cross_references.independent_of: EXP-190 (parked Scoreboard) — this lightweight module pulls existing prose into a structured surface and ships standalone. EXP-190 can subsume it later if/when it lands.

hero_composition_check.above_fold_constraint: Operator constraint (HNOTE-007 Item 1): thesis sentence and stat row both stay above the fold on a 1440x900 desktop viewport. Adding the falsification module below the bar chart pushes the accuracy-math-section and downstream content further down. The bar chart itself is ~6 rows × ~1.6rem each ≈ 14rem tall plus heading and caption ≈ 16rem. The falsification module is ~4 rows × ~3.5rem each ≈ 14rem tall plus heading and caption ≈ 16rem. Combined hero block (thesis-hero + bar chart + falsification module) is approximately 4rem (thesis) + 16rem (bars) + 16rem (module) = ~36rem ≈ 576px at default 16px root. With title-block above (~12rem) and viewport at 900px desktop, the falsification module sits AT or just BELOW the fold, not above. This matches the HNOTE's "above the fold OR just below the fold" guidance — the module is the first thing a reader scrolling sees after the bar chart, which is the reading rhythm the HNOTE describes.
hero_composition_check.mobile_consideration: On 600px-wide mobile, the .fm-row collapses to a 2-column grid (condition + status) with desc on a second row. Each row is ~5rem tall on mobile, so total module height ~22rem ≈ 352px. Module is well below fold on mobile — same as the bar chart — so no fold competition.
hero_composition_check.no_redundancy: Module does NOT restate any number that the verdict bar chart shows. Bar chart shows verdict counts (Refuted N, Misleading N, etc.); module shows criterion-progress (0/69, 1 partial, 0/69, pending). Different axes; complementary not redundant.

contents_added: fm-jump link to eg-falsifiability for full criteria
load_bearing_content_audit.contents_removed: NONE — this is a pure additive insertion. eg-falsifiability section in the Evaluation Guide is unchanged. The four conditions stay in their current full-prose form on that tab; the module surfaces them on the Overview as a persistent reminder per F3 / R3 reviewer recommendation.
load_bearing_content_audit.no_invented_conditions: The four module rows are verbatim distillations of the existing eg-falsifiability conditions. No new conditions were invented per HNOTE explicit boundary ("draw from existing Evaluation Guide content; do NOT invent new conditions").

kernel_of_truth: The "no falsifiability list" critique would be the easy bust: the dome could just respond "you say it's unfalsifiable, here is what would change my mind." We disarm that by showing exactly what would change our mind. The falsification module makes our position cooperative-appearing while keeping the bar high. The KERNEL the dome could plausibly seize on: "Conditions 1 and 3 are biased toward standard physics — anything that differs from standard physics will tend to be wrong." We address this in the existing eg-falsifiability prose (the "single discriminating prediction would be a genuine breakthrough" framing). The module surfaces the standard without re-arguing it — readers who want the full case follow the fm-jump link.
why_kernel_doesnt_break_module: The four conditions are all of the form "do X without smuggling in Y" — they leave room for genuine novel predictions. They do not require agreement with standard physics; they require differing FROM standard physics on a SPECIFIC discriminating observable, then confirming that difference with data. A correct dome prediction that differs from standard physics and is confirmed would meet condition 1 and we would upgrade. The module is therefore not a circular standard.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why a separate module? The bar chart already shows "0 confirmed" verdicts.
anticipated_objections.response: The bar chart shows verdict distribution across the 71 catalog entries, but does not state the criteria for what would CHANGE that distribution. Reviewers (round-1 R3, round-2 #3) repeatedly flagged that the falsification criteria sit on a different tab and casual readers never see them. The module surfaces them above-the-fold-adjacent on every Overview load. Different question, different surface.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not a tooltip or hover on the verdict pills?
anticipated_objections.response: Out of scope per HNOTE: "Tooltip on hover as an alternative to click-through (decided click-through is the cleaner pattern per F3 recommendation; hover is fragile on mobile)." The module is a persistent click-through surface — same pattern.

anticipated_objections.objection: Status values are subjective — "1 partial" for the eclipse is generous.
anticipated_objections.response: The "1 partial" framing is grounded in the existing eg-falsifiability text: "approach this standard but have methodological issues documented in our Timestamp Error analysis." This is "approaches but does not yet meet" — captured precisely by "partial". The sub-status "eclipse Aug 12, 2026" pinpoints which prediction is being credited. Curmudgeon may stress-test whether "partial" should be "pending" instead — defensible either way; we chose "partial" to acknowledge the dome's real (if methodologically flawed) effort rather than dismiss it.

anticipated_objections.objection: OPEN-001 sub-status for condition 4 cites a dome-side problem ID — readers may not know what OPEN-001 means.
anticipated_objections.response: The sub-status is paired with "pending" (full meaning visible). For readers who want context, the fm-jump link goes to eg-falsifiability where condition 4 prose explains V12/V13 takes WGS84 input. Section 1.3 also documents OPEN-001 directly. The sub-status is a concise hook, not a load-bearing claim.

anticipated_objections.objection: The module duplicates content from the Evaluation Guide tab — same conditions appear twice.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — that's the point. The reviewer's F3/R3 recommendation is that the falsification taxonomy should be reinforced wherever a verdict is referenced, not isolated on one tab. Persistent overview presence is the design intent. The Evaluation Guide tab keeps the full prose with all qualifications; the Overview module is the headline surface. Same data, different surface, different reading mode.

holes_addressed: Round-2 designer recommendation #3: persistent "What would change our verdict" overview module
Round-1 reviewer recommendation R3: same item, prior round
Principle #3 from external review: "the taxonomy of falsification criteria is the second-most-transportable idea after the verdict taxonomy itself; it should be visible from the Overview"
Newsworthy gap: the previous Overview did not state ANY falsification criteria above the fold; readers had to navigate to the Evaluation Guide tab to find them
new_evidence_added: No new factual claims. All four conditions and their status values are verbatim distillations of existing eg-falsifiability content. The fm-jump link routes readers to the full prose for context.

decider_instructions.integration_step_1: Apply FM1 (HTML insertion) via exact_string_replace in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Verify with: grep -c "class=\"falsifiability-module\"" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c "class=\"fm-row\"" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 4).
decider_instructions.integration_step_2: Apply FM2 (CSS insertion) via exact_string_replace. Verify with: grep -c ".falsifiability-module{margin:1.4rem" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c ".fm-status-zero" build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1).
decider_instructions.integration_step_3: Run node build.js html. Verify docs/index.html now contains class="falsifiability-module" exactly once.
decider_instructions.integration_step_4: Visual check: open docs/index.html, confirm the module renders below the verdict bar chart on the Overview tab. Confirm 4 rows, status colors (red zero / blue partial / gray pending) display correctly in light and dark mode.
decider_instructions.integration_step_5: Run tests/run.sh — expect existing tests to pass plus the new assertions added below. If existing tests fail with the module added, investigate (likely a positional assertion that needs update); do NOT loosen tests.
test_additions: Assert docs/index.html contains class="falsifiability-module" exactly once
Assert docs/index.html contains exactly 4 elements with class="fm-row"

Assert docs/index.html contains all four condition titles ("Discriminating prediction from dome geometry", "Genuine prospective prediction", "Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry", "Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs")
Assert the fm-jump anchor href="#eg-falsifiability" exists in docs/index.html
Assert the falsifiability-module section appears AFTER the verdict-bars section in docs/index.html (positional check via indexOf comparison)
decider_instructions.rollback_plan: If integration causes a build break or test failure that cannot be quickly resolved, revert the two patch_segments and document the failure mode in monitor/decisions/. The module is purely additive (no removals) so reverting is a clean string-delete.

curmudgeon_review_focus: Stress-test the four conditions: is the distillation faithful to the eg-falsifiability prose? Any wording that softens or sharpens beyond what the source supports?

Stress-test the status values: are "0 / 69", "1 partial", "0 / 69", "pending" defensible against the existing prose? Is "1 partial" too generous given the Timestamp Error caveat? Is "pending" right for condition 4 or should it be "0 / 69" given V13 has been published and tested?
Visual hierarchy: does the module compete with the thesis-hero (EXP-255) for attention, or does it complement it? Does it push the accuracy-math-section too far down?
Mobile composition: at 600px viewport, does the 2-column collapsed layout still read clearly? Are the .fm-status-num colors distinguishable on dark mode?
Check whether the module duplicates the verdict bar chart in any way that creates reader confusion

build_impact.expected_html_diff: New <section class="falsifiability-module"> with 4 <li class="fm-row"> children inserted after the existing <section class="verdict-bars">. ~25 lines added. No existing content removed.
build_impact.expected_css_diff: One long CSS rule string appended after the .accuracy-math-section @media rule. ~1700 chars added. No existing CSS modified.
build_impact.pdf_pipeline: Playwright headless Chromium loads docs/index.html via file:// — the module renders to PDF automatically with the same CSS. No DOCX pipeline (deprecated per EXP-250).

acceptance_criteria: falsifiability-module renders below verdict-bars on the Overview tab
4 rows display with condition titles, descriptions, and status values
fm-jump link navigates to eg-falsifiability in the Evaluation Guide tab
No regression on existing Overview content (verdict bar chart, scorecard sc-hero or thesis-hero per EXP-255 status, accuracy-math-section, downloads)
Light and dark mode both render correctly
Mobile viewport (600px) collapses to 2-column layout cleanly
All status colors derive from existing CSS tokens (no hardcoded hex)
out_of_scope_explicit: EXP-190 full Scoreboard schema work (parked, separate)
Evaluation Guide tab content rewrite (HNOTE explicit out-of-scope; only fm-jump link added)
Item 4 (How-to-Evaluate 5-question preface) — separate EXP recommended for next analyst run
Re-litigation of EXP-255 hero swap — strict additive insertion, anchor unaffected
New falsification conditions beyond the four in eg-falsifiability — HNOTE explicit boundary
newsworthy: Overview now states above-the-fold what would actually change our minds. Four concrete conditions, each with current status, visible without navigating to a separate tab. A reviewer cannot any longer claim the review hides its falsification criteria.
notes_for_decider: Two patch segments. Both anchors verified unique on the canonical (pre-EXP-255) generate-html.js. Anchors remain unique post-EXP-255 (EXP-255 modifies only the four-up scorecard block, not the bar chart call line, not the .accuracy-math-section CSS rule). Recommend integrating EXP-255 first per HNOTE priority, then EXP-256 second; either order produces a valid build.
tldr_evidence: The Evaluation Guide already lists four falsification criteria but they sit on a separate tab. Reviewers in two rounds asked for a persistent overview surface that shows what would change our mind. This EXP adds a 4-row module right below the verdict bar chart, drawing verbatim from the existing criteria.
tldr_verdict: Pure additive insertion: thesis on top, distribution in the middle, falsification criteria below — the Overview now reads as a complete claim / result / standard triptych. No invented conditions, no content removal, all colors via design tokens.
